message.content: I tried installing npm g @code-insights/cli multiple times even after uninstalling existing one, but still it downloads and installs v3.0.0 not 3.0.2 which is the latest

content.thinking: The user is having trouble with npm installing v3.0.0 instead of v3.0.2 of @code-insights/cli. Let me help diagnose this issue. Let me check the package.json to see what version is currently set, and also check the npm registry to see what's published.

content.text: Let me check a few things to diagnose this.

input.description: Check published versions on npm registry

content.text:
The npm registry shows **3.0.2 is published and tagged as `latest`**. So the issue is on your local side — npm is likely serving a cached version.

Try these in order:

**1. Clear npm cache and reinstall:**
```bash
npm cache clean --force
npm install -g @code-insights/cli
```

**2. If that still gives 3.0.0, force the version explicitly:**
```bash
npm install -g @code-insights/cli@3.0.2
```

**3. Check if an old version is lingering somewhere else in your PATH:**
```bash
which code-insights
code-insights --version
```

The most common cause is npm's local cache serving the stale 3.0.0 tarball. The `--force` cache clean should resolve it.

file.content:
# Changelog

All notable changes to `@code-insights/cli` will be documented in this file.

## [3.0.0] - 2026-02-28

See [MIGRATION.md](../MIGRATION.md) for the full upgrade guide from v2.

### Breaking Changes

- **Firebase removed** — No Firestore sync, no Firebase credentials, no service account required. All data is stored locally in SQLite at `~/.code-insights/data.db`.
- **`connect` command removed** — Generated Firebase connection URLs. No longer needed.
- **`init` config format changed** — v2 config had Firebase credentials fields. v3 config has only sync settings and optional LLM config. Re-run `code-insights init` after upgrading.
- **Data source changed to SQLite** — v2 wrote to Firestore. v3 writes to local SQLite. Existing Firestore data is not migrated. Re-sync with `code-insights sync --force`.
- **Hosted dashboard removed** — The dashboard at code-insights.app is no longer maintained.

### Added

- **`dashboard` command** — Starts a local Hono server and opens the built-in React SPA at `localhost:7890`. Replaces the hosted dashboard.
- **Embedded Vite + React SPA** — Full browser dashboard for session browsing, analytics, and insights. No external URL required.
- **Server-side LLM analysis** — API keys are stored and used server-side. No key exposure to the browser.
- **Multi-tool support** — Session providers for Cursor, Codex CLI, and Copilot CLI (in addition to Claude Code).
- **`config llm` command** — Interactive and non-interactive LLM provider configuration (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, Ollama).
- **`--source <tool>` flag on `sync`** — Sync only from a specific tool.
- **`--verbose` flag on `sync`** — Show diagnostic warnings from providers.
- **`--regenerate-titles` flag on `sync`** — Regenerate session titles from content.
- **`--no-sync` flag on `stats`** — Skip auto-sync before displaying analytics.

### Changed

- **`init`** — No longer requires Firebase credentials. Sets up local SQLite database and config only.
- **`open`** — Now opens `localhost:7890` (local dashboard server) instead of code-insights.app.
- **`reset`** — Clears local SQLite database and sync state instead of Firestore data.
- **`sync`** — Writes to local SQLite instead of Firestore.
- **`status`** — Reports SQLite session counts and local sync state.

## [2.1.0] - 2026-02-27

### Added
- **CopilotProvider** — VS Code Copilot Chat session support (`sourceTool: 'copilot'`)

### Fixed

- **`--version` now reads from package.json** — Previously hardcoded as `3.0.0`, causing `code-insights --version` to always report `3.0.0` regardless of installed version.

### Fixed

- **Added missing server runtime dependencies** — `hono` and `@hono/node-server` added to CLI package.json so `code-insights dashboard` works for npm-installed users.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Changelog

All notable changes to `@code-insights/cli` will be documented in this file.

## [3.0.0] - 2026-02-28

See [MIGRATION.md](../MIGRATION.md) for the full upgrade guide from v2.

### Breaking Changes

- **Firebase removed** — No Firestore sync, no Firebase credentials, no service account required. All data is stored locally in SQLite at `~/.code-insights/data.db`.
- **`connect` command removed** — Generated Firebase connection URLs. No longer needed.
- **`init` config format changed** — v2 config had Firebase credentials fields. v3 config has only sync settings and optional LLM config. Re-run `code-insights init` after upgrading.
- **Data source changed to SQLite** — v2 wrote to Firestore. v3 writes to local SQLite. Existing Firestore data is not migrated. Re-sync with `code-insights sync --force`.
- **Hosted dashboard removed** — The dashboard at code-insights.app is no longer maintained.

### Added

- **`dashboard` command** — Starts a local Hono server and opens the built-in React SPA at `localhost:7890`. Replaces the hosted dashboard.
- **Embedded Vite + React SPA** — Full browser dashboard for session browsing, analytics, and insights. No external URL required.
- **Server-side LLM analysis** — API keys are stored and used server-side. No key exposure to the browser.
- **Multi-tool support** — Session providers for Cursor, Codex CLI, and Copilot CLI (in addition to Claude Code).
- **`config llm` command** — Interactive and non-interactive LLM provider configuration (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, Ollama).
- **`--source <tool>` flag on `sync`** — Sync only from a specific tool.
- **`--verbose` flag on `sync`** — Show diagnostic warnings from providers.
- **`--regenerate-titles` flag on `sync`** — Regenerate session titles from content.
- **`--no-sync` flag on `stats`** — Skip auto-sync before displaying analytics.

### Changed

- **`init`** — No longer requires Firebase credentials. Sets up local SQLite database and config only.
- **`open`** — Now opens `localhost:7890` (local dashboard server) instead of code-insights.app.
- **`reset`** — Clears local SQLite database and sync state instead of Firestore data.
- **`sync`** — Writes to local SQLite instead of Firestore.
- **`status`** — Reports SQLite session counts and local sync state.

## [2.1.0] - 2026-02-27

### Added
- **CopilotProvider** — VS Code Copilot Chat session support (`sourceTool: 'copilot'`)
- **`status` command multi-tool summary** — displays session counts broken down by source tool (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI, Copilot)
- **`reset` now clears `stats` collection** — ensures the `stats/usage` document is wiped on full reset

## [2.0.0] - 2026-02-26

### Added
- **Terminal analytics suite** — `stats`, `stats cost`, `stats projects`, `stats today`, `stats models` commands
- **Multi-tool support** — Cursor, Codex CLI, and Copilot CLI session providers
- **Zero-config mode** — Local-first stats without Firebase setup
- **`open` command** — Launch web dashboard from terminal
- **Anonymous telemetry** — Opt-out usage analytics (`telemetry` command)
- **Contextual tips** — Post-command suggestions and one-time welcome message
- **Fuzzy project matching** — `--project` flag uses Levenshtein distance matching

### Fixed
- Improved error messages for unconfigured commands
- Cross-platform path handling (experimental Windows support)

### Changed
- `init` defaults to local data source (Firebase optional)
- `firebase` config field is now optional for backward compatibility

## [1.0.2] - 2026-02-20

Initial public release with Claude Code session parsing, Firestore sync, and web dashboard integration.

lines: All notable changes to `@code-insights/cli` will be documented in this file.
+- **`--version` now reads from package.json** — Previously hardcoded as `3.0.0`, causing `code-insights --version` to always report `3.0.0` regardless of installed version.
+- **Added missing server runtime dependencies** — `hono` and `@hono/node-server` added to CLI package.json so `code-insights dashboard` works for npm-installed users.
See [MIGRATION.md](../MIGRATION.md) for the full upgrade guide from v2.

All notable changes to `@code-insights/cli` will be documented in this file.

## [3.0.0] - 2026-02-28

See [MIGRATION.md](../MIGRATION.md) for the full upgrade guide from v2.

### Breaking Changes

{
  "name": "@code-insights/cli",
  "version": "3.0.2",
  "description": "AI coding session analytics with built-in dashboard",
  "type": "module",
  "main": "dist/index.js",
  "exports": {
    ".": "./dist/index.js",
    "./db/client": "./dist/db/client.js",
    "./db/read": "./dist/db/read.js",
    "./db/write": "./dist/db/write.js",
    "./db/schema": "./dist/db/schema.js",
    "./types": "./dist/types.js",
    "./utils/config": "./dist/utils/config.js",
    "./utils/browser": "./dist/utils/browser.js",
    "./constants/llm-providers": "./dist/constants/llm-providers.js",
    "./utils/telemetry": "./dist/utils/telemetry.js"
  },
  "bin": {
    "code-insights": "./dist/index.js"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "build": "tsc",
    "dev": "tsc --watch",
    "start": "node dist/index.js",
    "prepublishOnly": "cd .. && pnpm -r build && cp -r dashboard/dist cli/dashboard-dist && cp -r server/dist cli/server-dist"
  },
  "files": [
    "dist",
    "dashboard-dist",
    "server-dist",
    "README.md",
    "CHANGELOG.md",
    "LICENSE"
  ],
  "engines": {
    "node": ">=18.0.0"
  },
  "keywords": [
    "claude",
    "claude-code",
    "cursor",
    "codex",
    "copilot",
    "ai-coding",
    "insights",
    "analytics",
    "sqlite",
    "local-first"
  ],
  "author": "Srikanth Rao M",
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "git+https://github.com/melagiri/code-insights.git",
    "directory": "cli"
  },
  "bugs": {
    "url": "https://github.com/melagiri/code-insights/issues"
  },
  "homepage": "https://github.com/melagiri/code-insights/tree/master/cli#readme",
  "publishConfig": {
    "access": "public"
  },
  "license": "MIT",
  "dependencies": {
    "@hono/node-server": "^1.13.7",
    "better-sqlite3": "^12.6.2",
    "chalk": "^5.4.1",
    "commander": "^12.1.0",
    "hono": "^4.7.4",
    "inquirer": "^12.6.1",
    "ora": "^8.2.0"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/better-sqlite3": "^7.6.13",
    "@types/node": "^22.15.30",
    "typescript": "^5.9.3"
  }
}

lines: "description": "AI coding session analytics with built-in dashboard",

input.new_string:
## [3.0.3] - 2026-02-28

### Fixed

- **`--version` now reads from package.json** — Previously hardcoded as `3.0.0`, causing `code-insights --version` to always report `3.0.0` regardless of installed version.

## [3.0.2] - 2026-02-28

### Fixed

- **Added missing server runtime dependencies** — `hono` and `@hono/node-server` added to CLI package.json so `code-insights dashboard` works for npm-installed users.

## [3.0.0] - 2026-02-28

content.content: The file /Users/melagiri/Workspace/codeInsights/code-insights/.claude/worktrees/version-bump-3.0.3/cli/CHANGELOG.md has been updated successfully.

toolUseResult.newString:
## [3.0.3] - 2026-02-28

### Fixed

- **`--version` now reads from package.json** — Previously hardcoded as `3.0.0`, causing `code-insights --version` to always report `3.0.0` regardless of installed version.

## [3.0.2] - 2026-02-28

### Fixed

- **Added missing server runtime dependencies** — `hono` and `@hono/node-server` added to CLI package.json so `code-insights dashboard` works for npm-installed users.

## [3.0.0] - 2026-02-28
toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Changelog

All notable changes to `@code-insights/cli` will be documented in this file.

## [3.0.0] - 2026-02-28

See [MIGRATION.md](../MIGRATION.md) for the full upgrade guide from v2.

### Breaking Changes

- **Firebase removed** — No Firestore sync, no Firebase credentials, no service account required. All data is stored locally in SQLite at `~/.code-insights/data.db`.
- **`connect` command removed** — Generated Firebase connection URLs. No longer needed.
- **`init` config format changed** — v2 config had Firebase credentials fields. v3 config has only sync settings and optional LLM config. Re-run `code-insights init` after upgrading.
- **Data source changed to SQLite** — v2 wrote to Firestore. v3 writes to local SQLite. Existing Firestore data is not migrated. Re-sync with `code-insights sync --force`.
- **Hosted dashboard removed** — The dashboard at code-insights.app is no longer maintained.

### Added

- **`dashboard` command** — Starts a local Hono server and opens the built-in React SPA at `localhost:7890`. Replaces the hosted dashboard.
- **Embedded Vite + React SPA** — Full browser dashboard for session browsing, analytics, and insights. No external URL required.
- **Server-side LLM analysis** — API keys are stored and used server-side. No key exposure to the browser.
- **Multi-tool support** — Session providers for Cursor, Codex CLI, and Copilot CLI (in addition to Claude Code).
- **`config llm` command** — Interactive and non-interactive LLM provider configuration (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, Ollama).
- **`--source <tool>` flag on `sync`** — Sync only from a specific tool.
- **`--verbose` flag on `sync`** — Show diagnostic warnings from providers.
- **`--regenerate-titles` flag on `sync`** — Regenerate session titles from content.
- **`--no-sync` flag on `stats`** — Skip auto-sync before displaying analytics.

### Changed

- **`init`** — No longer requires Firebase credentials. Sets up local SQLite database and config only.
- **`open`** — Now opens `localhost:7890` (local dashboard server) instead of code-insights.app.
- **`reset`** — Clears local SQLite database and sync state instead of Firestore data.
- **`sync`** — Writes to local SQLite instead of Firestore.
- **`status`** — Reports SQLite session counts and local sync state.

## [2.1.0] - 2026-02-27

### Added
- **CopilotProvider** — VS Code Copilot Chat session support (`sourceTool: 'copilot'`)
- **`status` command multi-tool summary** — displays session counts broken down by source tool (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI, Copilot)
- **`reset` now clears `stats` collection** — ensures the `stats/usage` document is wiped on full reset

## [2.0.0] - 2026-02-26

### Added
- **Terminal analytics suite** — `stats`, `stats cost`, `stats projects`, `stats today`, `stats models` commands
- **Multi-tool support** — Cursor, Codex CLI, and Copilot CLI session providers
- **Zero-config mode** — Local-first stats without Firebase setup
- **`open` command** — Launch web dashboard from terminal
- **Anonymous telemetry** — Opt-out usage analytics (`telemetry` command)
- **Contextual tips** — Post-command suggestions and one-time welcome message
- **Fuzzy project matching** — `--project` flag uses Levenshtein distance matching

### Fixed
- Improved error messages for unconfigured commands
- Cross-platform path handling (experimental Windows support)

### Changed
- `init` defaults to local data source (Firebase optional)
- `firebase` config field is now optional for backward compatibility

## [1.0.2] - 2026-02-20

Initial public release with Claude Code session parsing, Firestore sync, and web dashboard integration.

lines: All notable changes to `@code-insights/cli` will be documented in this file.
+- **`--version` now reads from package.json** — Previously hardcoded as `3.0.0`, causing `code-insights --version` to always report `3.0.0` regardless of installed version.
+- **Added missing server runtime dependencies** — `hono` and `@hono/node-server` added to CLI package.json so `code-insights dashboard` works for npm-installed users.
See [MIGRATION.md](../MIGRATION.md) for the full upgrade guide from v2.

content.text: All edits applied. Now let me build, verify, commit, and publish.

content.text: Version reads correctly. Now commit and push to master.

content.content: worktree-version-bump-3.0.3

input.command:
git add cli/src/index.ts cli/package.json cli/CHANGELOG.md && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix: read CLI version from package.json instead of hardcoded string

`code-insights --version` was always reporting 3.0.0 regardless of
installed version because it was hardcoded. Now reads dynamically
from package.json.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
